arguments.task: Visit the official WooCommerce documentation pages and extract evidence for: 1) REST API availability and scope, 2) authentication methods for WooCommerce REST API, 3) any rate limit documentation, 4) access/pricing requirements for WooCommerce core or API usage, and 5) any official mention of MCP or agent tooling. Prioritize first-party pages such as developer docs, docs.woocommerce.com/woocommerce.com docs, GitHub under the WooCommerce org, pricing pages, and support docs. Return the exact URLs used plus concise findings.

input.thought: Open first-party WooCommerce documentation and extract evidence for buildability assessment.

raw_result.description: Open-source ecommerce platform for WordPress with REST and Store APIs for managing store data and storefront interactions.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official REST API authentication docs describe Basic Authentication over HTTPS using consumer key/secret, and OAuth 1.0a signing for HTTP or when query-string auth is needed. Store API is unauthenticated for many read endpoints and can use nonce/cart tokens for protected store interactions; classified here as 'other' because it is not one of the standard schema auth enums.
raw_result.gating_reason: WooCommerce is free and open source, and its official developer/API documentation is publicly available without sales or partner approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public first-party REST API documentation covers core commerce resources (orders, products, customers, coupons, reports, taxes, shipping, webhooks, settings, system status, etc.) and separate Store API docs cover storefront/cart/checkout flows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official WooCommerce MCP documentation was verified in the gathered first-party evidence. This may still exist in third-party repositories, so human review is advised if MCP availability is critical.

raw_result.blocker: Authentication and endpoint behavior depend on a user-owned/self-hosted WordPress + WooCommerce store, and the verified rate-limit documentation gathered was for the Store API rather than clearly for the administrative REST API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Verified first-party rate-limit page applies to Store API endpoints: rate limiting is disabled by default and can be enabled, with documented defaults of 25 requests per 10 seconds and limit headers. No clearly verified admin REST API global rate-limit policy was gathered.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs show the REST API was integrated into the WordPress REST API since WooCommerce 2.6; there is a separate Store API; auth docs explicitly distinguish HTTPS Basic Auth from OAuth 1.0a usage; public docs are detailed and first-party.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP existence was not verified, and the gathered rate-limit evidence is specifically for the Store API, leaving ambiguity about limits for the broader administrative REST API.

result.description: Open-source ecommerce platform for WordPress with REST and Store APIs for managing store data and storefront interactions.
result.auth_notes: Official REST API authentication docs describe Basic Authentication over HTTPS using consumer key/secret, and OAuth 1.0a signing for HTTP or when query-string auth is needed. Store API is unauthenticated for many read endpoints and can use nonce/cart tokens for protected store interactions; classified here as 'other' because it is not one of the standard schema auth enums.
result.gating_reason: WooCommerce is free and open source, and its official developer/API documentation is publicly available without sales or partner approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public first-party REST API documentation covers core commerce resources (orders, products, customers, coupons, reports, taxes, shipping, webhooks, settings, system status, etc.) and separate Store API docs cover storefront/cart/checkout flows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official WooCommerce MCP documentation was verified in the gathered first-party evidence. This may still exist in third-party repositories, so human review is advised if MCP availability is critical.

result.blocker: Authentication and endpoint behavior depend on a user-owned/self-hosted WordPress + WooCommerce store, and the verified rate-limit documentation gathered was for the Store API rather than clearly for the administrative REST API.
result.rate_limit_notes: Verified first-party rate-limit page applies to Store API endpoints: rate limiting is disabled by default and can be enabled, with documented defaults of 25 requests per 10 seconds and limit headers. No clearly verified admin REST API global rate-limit policy was gathered.
result.notable_signals: Official docs show the REST API was integrated into the WordPress REST API since WooCommerce 2.6; there is a separate Store API; auth docs explicitly distinguish HTTPS Basic Auth from OAuth 1.0a usage; public docs are detailed and first-party.
result.human_review_reason: MCP existence was not verified, and the gathered rate-limit evidence is specifically for the Store API, leaving ambiguity about limits for the broader administrative REST API.
